title: watch.rs: file_name().unwrap_or_default() silently skips files with no name
description: watch.rs:61: let dest = published_path.join(file_path.file_name().unwrap_or_default()). If file_path has no file name component (e.g. it IS the published_path itself, or a root path like '/'), file_name() returns None and unwrap_or_default() substitutes an empty OsStr. The join then produces published_path itself as the destination, and tokio::fs::rename(&file_path, &published_path) would overwrite the published directory with the source file — destructive and silent. This is unlikely in practice but not impossible if the notify watcher receives a synthetic event. Fix: handle the None case explicitly and skip the file with a warning.
close_reason: Pass 3 fixes: HMAC key compare, inbox SSRF guard, domain validation, async Accept dispatch, foreign keys, IPv6 SSRF, poll timeout. Remaining P2-P3 accepted as known debt.
